What We Monitor

If it runs on an AC motor, SAM4 monitors it.

AC induction motors, centrifugal and positive displacement pumps, compressors, fans, conveyors, and many other rotating equipment types. SAM4 monitors the full drivetrain from the electrical cabinet. Over 10,000 assets monitored across 80+ customers and 5 continents. Here's what we cover and what we detect.

Talk to an engineer See Asset Types

Asset types

Every AC-driven rotating asset

SAM4 monitors any asset driven by an AC motor. Current and voltage signatures captured at the motor control cabinet reveal faults across the entire drivetrain: motor, coupling, gearbox, and driven load.

Centrifugal pumps

Most monitored

Submersible, booster, borehole, process, injection, transfer, and circulation pumps. SAM4 detects bearing degradation, cavitation, blockage, seal wear, imbalance, and efficiency loss (weeks to months before failure).

Blockage Cavitation Imbalance more
Pump monitoring details →

Fans & blowers

Ventilation fans, cooling fans, process blowers, and aerators. SAM4 detects imbalance, bearing degradation, belt issues, and process deviations. Critical for maintaining safe atmospheric conditions in enclosed spaces.

Imbalance Belt wear Bearing degradation more
Fan & blower monitoring details →

Conveyors

Belt conveyors, screw conveyors, and chain conveyors. SAM4 detects bearing degradation, misalignment, belt/chain issues, blockage, and overload conditions. Common in materials handling across mining, pulp, and manufacturing.

Bearing degradation Misalignment Blockage more
Conveyor monitoring details →

Agitators & mixers

Chemical reactors, blending vessels, and mixing tanks. SAM4 detects bearing degradation, seal failure indicators, imbalance from product buildup, and gearbox issues without opening the vessel or entering the process area.

Bearing degradation Gearbox faults Imbalance more
Agitator & mixer monitoring details →

Compressors

Gas compressors, air compressors, and process compressors, including assets in ATEX/hazardous zones. SAM4 detects bearing wear, valve faults, imbalance, and efficiency degradation without accessing the compressor skid.

Bearing wear Valve faults Imbalance more
Compressor monitoring details →

Archimedes screws

Archimedes screw pumps used in water treatment inlet works and low-head pumping stations. SAM4 detects bearing degradation, trough wear indicators, drive chain faults, and efficiency loss. This is critical for preventing pollution events.

Bearing degradation Trough wear Drive chain faults more
Archimedes screw monitoring details →

Electrical submersible pumps (ESPs)

Downhole ESPs in oil & gas production. These assets operate thousands of metres underground and cannot be inspected without pulling the string (a process costing days and hundreds of thousands of dollars. SAM4 detects motor degradation, pump wear, and gas lock from the surface VSD.

Motor degradation Pump wear Gas lock more
ESP monitoring details →

Canned motor pumps

Sealless pumps where the motor rotor is immersed in the process fluid. Impossible to access with vibration sensors. SAM4 detects bearing degradation, rotor eccentricity, internal recirculation changes, and winding faults: the only practical condition monitoring approach for these assets.

Bearing degradation Rotor eccentricity Winding faults more
Canned motor pump monitoring details →

Transmissions

Gearboxes, couplings, belt drives, and chain drives. SAM4 detects faults across the full mechanical transmission path: from the motor through to the driven equipment). SAM4 uses electrical signature analysis at the MCC.

Gear wear Misalignment Belt slip more
Transmission monitoring details →

Low voltage motors

AC induction motors up to 1kV: the backbone of industrial operations. SAM4 monitors the motor itself plus the entire connected drivetrain, detecting winding faults, bearing degradation, eccentricity, misalignment, and electrical supply issues from the MCC.

Bearing degradation Winding faults Misalignment more
LV motor monitoring details →

Medium voltage motors

AC motors from 1kV to 15kV driving critical process equipment. These high-value assets are expensive to replace and hard to monitor with sensors. SAM4 detects winding insulation degradation, rotor bar faults, partial discharge indicators, and bearing wear (all from the motor control centre) control centre.

Winding insulation Rotor bar faults Partial discharge more
MV motor monitoring details →

Asset not listed?

If it runs on an AC motor, we can likely monitor it. Our engineers have deployed SAM4 on dozens of specialist asset types not listed here. Get in touch to discuss your specific equipment.

Talk to an Engineer

Seal leaks and bearing failures start with detectable root causes

Mechanical seal leaks and bearing failures are rarely spontaneous. They are accelerated by misalignment, mechanical unbalance, and cavitation) are conditions that SAM4 detects with high confidence. Clients monitoring these upstream conditions experience measurably lower occurrence of seal and bearing failures.

100% Misalignment detection
96.3% Mechanical unbalance detection
86.7% Cavitation detection

Fleet experience

10,000+ assets. Dozens of industries. Five continents.

10,000+ assets monitored globally
80+ customers
5,300+ validated findings delivered
5 continents
See customer results →

Detection boundaries

What SAM4 detects well. Where it has limits

No condition monitoring technology detects everything. We publish our detection boundaries so you know exactly what to expect. Based on 1,874 resolved incidents from January 2024 to February 2026.

Strong detection (>85%)

Blockage / clogging: 97.4% detection across 545 cases
Mechanical unbalance: 96.3% detection across 54 cases
Belt degradation: 89.7% detection across 39 cases
Cavitation: 86.7% detection across 15 cases
Misalignment: 100% detection across 5 cases

Known limitations

Motor bearing degradation: subtler ESA signatures than driven equipment bearings. Accounts for most bearing detection misses.
Stator shorts: too localised for current ESA sensitivity. Outside SAM4 detection scope.
Compressor-specific faults: reciprocating and variable-load profiles differ from SAM4's primary training set (37.5% detection).
Coupling unbalance: often masked by other drivetrain dynamics (57.1% detection).
Supply and cable faults: intermittent electrical anomalies are harder to classify consistently (50–53% detection).
See full detection transparency report → Learn how ESA works →
